name: ntfy
instructions: You are connected to the 'ntfy' host via rrsh, a read-only diagnostic gateway. Call list_commands first — the args_pattern regex on each rule tells you exactly which argv shapes are accepted. Rules whose 'as' field contains 'root' auto-elevate (implicit sudo) — you do NOT need to pass {"as": "root"} for those. Log readers (tail/head/cat/grep/wc), fail2ban-client, last, and lastb all run as root because the log files and auxiliary databases require it. No write access. If a command you want isn't allowed, stop and tell the operator rather than trying to work around the matcher.

commands.description: Show the effective username.
Show the user/group identity (uid, gid, groups).
Print the system hostname.
Show how long the system has been up and the load average.
Print kernel/OS info. Only `-a` is permitted.
List processes. Permitted args: `aux`, `auxf`, `-ef`, or `-eo <format>`.
Take one batch-mode snapshot of top (`-bn1`).
Show the process tree.
Report memory usage. Optional `-h`, `-m`, or `-g`; no args also OK.
Report filesystem space usage. Optional `-h` or `-i`; no args also OK.
Summarize disk usage under /var/log (or a subpath of it). Must be `-sh <path>`.
List block devices.
Show network state. Permitted: `addr`, `route`, `link`, `link show`.
List listening sockets with PIDs. Permitted: `-tlnp`, `-ulnp`, `-tulnp`.
Ping a host a fixed number of times. Must be `-c <count> <host>`.
Perform DNS lookups.
Fetch a local HTTP/HTTPS URL (localhost only). Must be `-sS <url>`.
Show recent successful logins from /var/log/wtmp. Optional `-n <count>` or `-<count>`.
Show recent FAILED login attempts from /var/log/btmp. Useful for spotting SSH brute-force activity. Optional `-n <count>` or `-<count>`.
Inspect fail2ban state. `status` lists active jails; `status <jail>` (e.g. `status sshd` or `status nginx-400`) shows currently-banned IPs. Jail names may contain hyphens.
Read systemd journal for ntfy, nginx, ssh, or sshd. Patterns: `[--no-pager] -u <unit> [-n <count> | --since <expr>]`. Auto-elevated so journal entries from other users are visible.
Tail the last N lines of an ntfy, nginx, syslog, or auth.log file. Must be `-n <count> <path>`.
Read the first N lines of an ntfy, nginx, syslog, or auth.log file. Must be `-n <count> <path>`.
Read a file under /var/log/{ntfy,nginx} or /var/log/{syslog,auth.log}*.
List /var/log or a subpath. Must be `-l[a][h] <path>`.
Count lines in a log file under /var/log/{ntfy,nginx} or /var/log/{syslog,auth.log}*. Must be `-l <path>`.
Case-insensitive grep against a log file under /var/log/{ntfy,nginx} or /var/log/{syslog,auth.log}*. Must be `-i <pattern> <path>`.

commands.args: ^(status ntfy|status nginx|is-active ntfy|is-active nginx|list-timers|show ntfy|show nginx)$
commands.description: Inspect systemd units. Permitted: `status|is-active|show <ntfy|nginx>`, or `list-timers`.
Inspect ntfy state. Permitted: `user list`, `access list`, `tier list`.
